briefs.title: Council to Consider: Territorial Land Acknowledgement — Port Moody Meeting
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to read a Territorial Land Acknowledgement at its upcoming meeting, recognizing that the city operates on the ancestral and unceded — meaning never formally surrendered — homelands of eight Indigenous Peoples, including the Kwikwetlem, Tsleil-Waututh, Musqueam, and Squamish Nations, among others.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Closed-Door Session on Land, Staff, and Service Negotiations — Port Moody
briefs.summary: Port Moody's Special Council meeting on April 28, 2026 is set to move into a closed session — meaning the public will be asked to leave — to discuss sensitive topics including staff matters, land deals, labour relations, and early-stage negotiations about a possible new city service. These discussions are kept private under BC's Community Charter, a provincial law that sets the rules for how municipalities operate.
briefs.action_required: Watch for any public announcements after the meeting about land deals or new city services that could affect your business.
